title: Elements, attributes and nesting

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for Elements, attributes and nesting; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

canonicalExplanation.summary: HTML tokenization and tree construction interpret elements, attributes, and text into a document structure.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body:
Start with this case: The fragment

Hello friend

creates nested paragraph, emphasis, and text nodes. Your goal is to author and repair a small element tree without invalid nesting or duplicate attributes. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: This skill has no declared earlier lesson. The course assumes: Basic browser and file use. You can continue when those ideas feel familiar.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: HTML tokenization and tree construction interpret elements, attributes, and text into a document structure. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Parser error recovery exists but is not a substitute for conforming markup and can produce unexpected trees. This draft therefore rejects: HTML is executed line by line exactly like JavaScript source.

includes: Write valid start/end tags, void elements, quoted attributes and properly nested structures.
Author and repair a small element tree without invalid nesting or duplicate attributes.
excludes: Parser error recovery exists but is not a substitute for conforming markup and can produce unexpected trees.

outcomes: Author and repair a small element tree without invalid nesting or duplicate attributes.

walkthrough:
Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: The fragment

creates nested paragraph, emphasis, and text nodes.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: HTML is parsed into a document tree; author conforming markup and inspect the resulting DOM rather than relying on recovery.

recap.summary: You should now be able to author and repair a small element tree without invalid nesting or duplicate attributes. Keep this boundary in view: Parser error recovery exists but is not a substitute for conforming markup and can produce unexpected trees.
